text: AP reported a mixed June 18 oil settlement: Brent crude rose 0.4% to $79.85 per barrel, while US benchmark crude fell 0.2% to $75.85 per barrel.

market_session: reported before and during June 18 US session
text: AP described the geopolitical catalyst as an initial US-Iran agreement that took immediate effect, started a 60-day negotiating clock for final nuclear terms, and included sanctions waivers allowing Iran to sell oil freely.

The June 17 FOMC statement held the federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% by a 12-0 vote and cited Middle East uncertainty and energy-related supply shocks; this supports policy-risk context, not a direct causal attribution for a dot-plot shift.

text: AP reported that technology stocks had some of the biggest gains on June 18, with Intel up 10.6%, Nvidia up 3.0%, and Micron Technology up 8.7%.
NYSE and Nasdaq calendars list Friday, June 19, 2026 as a Juneteenth market holiday, making June 18 the final US equity trading session of the week.

value.offset: US-Iran interim peace plan limited downside
central_bank_context: Bank of England held Bank Rate at 3.75%
Swiss National Bank left policy rate unchanged

market_session: DPA/Yahoo European close wrap after the June 18 regular session
text: DPA's European close wrap said regional stocks closed broadly lower as interest-rate concerns after the Federal Reserve's hawkish tone weighed on sentiment, while news of a US-Iran interim peace plan helped limit the downside; investors also digested unchanged BoE and SNB policy decisions.

market_session: DPA/Yahoo European close wrap after the June 18 regular session
text: DPA's close wrap identified heavy dispersion: in the UK LSEG fell 7%, Persimmon 6.1%, Fresnillo 5.8%, and Endeavour Mining 5.7%; in Germany Infineon climbed about 6.5% and Siemens Energy 4.7% while Mercedes-Benz, SAP and BMW fell 4.0%-4.6%; in France Capgemini fell nearly 9%, while Edenred rose more than 17%, Legrand 5%, and STMicroelectronics and Kering about 4.5% each.

statement_notes: economic activity expanding at a solid pace despite elevated uncertainty partly owed to the Middle East conflict
inflation remains elevated relative to the 2 percent goal partly reflecting supply shocks including energy

text: The FOMC maintained the federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% on June 17, 2026 by a 12-0 vote, while stating that economic activity was expanding at a solid pace despite elevated uncertainty partly owed to the Middle East conflict and that inflation remained elevated partly because of supply shocks including energy.

calculation.method: counted 2026 federal-funds dots above the current 3.625 percent target-range midpoint and read median projection table

text: The June 2026 FOMC projection materials showed a 3.8% median end-2026 federal funds projection, with 9 of 18 June participants placing their 2026 dot above the current 3.625% midpoint, and median 2026 PCE/core PCE inflation projections of 3.6% and 3.3%.

calculation.method: Treasury 10Y par yield minus 2Y par yield, compared with the prior Treasury business day

text: The official U.S. Treasury par-yield curve on June 18, 2026 showed the 2-year yield at 4.19%, the 10-year at 4.46%, and the 30-year at 4.90%, with the 2s10s spread flattening to +27 basis points from +29 basis points on June 17.

subject.name: Bank of England Monetary Policy Committee

text: The Bank of England kept Bank Rate at 3.75% on June 18, 2026 by a 7-2 vote, with Megan Greene and Huw Pill preferring a 25 bp increase to 4.00%; the minutes put May CPI at 2.8%, projected CPI a little under 3% in Q3 and a little over 3.25% in Q4, and noted Brent around $79 and UK wholesale gas around 100 pence per therm immediately before the meeting.

The Swiss National Bank left its policy rate at 0.00% on June 18, 2026, kept the discount on sight deposits above the threshold at 0.25 percentage points, said May inflation had risen to 0.6% from 0.1% in February, and forecast annual inflation of 0.6% in 2026, 0.6% in 2027, and 0.7% in 2028 under a 0% policy-rate assumption while noting increased willingness to intervene in FX if necessary.

calculation.method: DOL seasonally adjusted weekly release

text: The U.S. Department of Labor reported June 18 that seasonally adjusted initial jobless claims fell by 4,000 to 226,000 for the week ending June 13, 2026, while the four-week average rose to 223,250 and insured unemployment increased by 24,000 to 1.810 million for the week ending June 6 with the insured unemployment rate unchanged at 1.2%.

calculation.method: vendor_reported_DXY_news_item

text: Trading Economics reported on June 18, 2026 that DXY increased to 100.72, its highest level since May 2025, with the Dollar Index up 1.39% over four weeks and 1.83% over 12 months.

calculation.method: financial_news_reported_futures_settlement_and_session_low

text: On June 18, 2026, NYMEX WTI for July delivery settled at $76.60/bbl, down $0.19 or 0.3% after a $73.58 intraday low, while August ICE Brent settled at $79.85/bbl, down $0.30 or 0.4% after a $76.54 intraday low.

value.us_blockade_status: lifted on Iranian ports on the strait

text: DTN reported June 18 that the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding appeared to assure immediate toll-free transit through Hormuz, a route that normally oversees about 20 million bpd of petroleum liquids, and that the U.S. military had lifted its naval blockade on Iranian ports on the strait while Goldman Sachs projected a 13 million bpd surge in transit flows and full normalization by late July.

calculation.method: wire_reported_close

text: Risk sentiment rebounded sharply on June 18, 2026: the S&P 500 rose 80.48 points, or 1.08%, to 7,500.58, erasing most of the prior session's 1.21% drop as geopolitical de-escalation and a chip-sector rally offset the hawkish Federal Reserve signal from June 17.

calculation.method: wire_reported_close

text: Growth and tech stocks led the rebound: the Nasdaq Composite surged 496.28 points, or 1.91%, to 26,517.93, the strongest advance among the major indexes, powered by a 6.4% rally in the Philadelphia semiconductor index after Intel shares jumped 10.6% to a record high on news of an Apple chip-design partnership.

calculation.method: wire_reported_close

text: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 72.15 points, or 0.14%, to 51,564.70, a comparatively muted advance that reflected the day's risk rotation into growth and semis rather than blue chips, with energy stocks dragging as oil prices fell on the U.S.-Iran peace agreement.

calculation.method: vendor_quote_daily_change

text: Small caps outperformed large caps: the Russell 2000 jumped 2.12% to 2,979.77, the best-performing major index on the day, as a modest decline in Treasury yields eased funding-cost pressure on rate-sensitive smaller companies even amid the Fed's hawkish tilt.

calculation.method: vendor_quote_daily_change

text: The Cboe Volatility Index plunged 2.04 points, or 11.06%, to close at 16.40 on June 18, 2026, unwinding nearly all of the prior session's 12.37% spike to 18.44 and signaling that the post-FOMC hedging bid dissipated as geopolitical risk premia collapsed on the U.S.-Iran peace deal.

Breadth was mixed and narrower than the headline rally suggests: five of 11 major S&P 500 industry sectors finished higher, with technology leading gainers while energy lagged as oil prices slid, contrasting with the prior session's uniformly negative all-11-red breadth.

The Philadelphia semiconductor index soared 6.4%, sharply outperforming the broad market, after President Trump said Apple had agreed to work with Intel to design and manufacture chips in the U.S., sending Intel shares up 10.6% to a record close and lifting the entire chip complex including SanDisk (+11.6%) and Super Micro (+10.1%).

The CNN Fear & Greed Index read 38 ('Fear') as of the June 18 close, up from 32 the previous day and 31 one week earlier, reflecting a market still in fear territory despite the day's rally — the index had not yet recovered to its prior-month reading of 61 ('Greed'), indicating that the rebound did not fully erase the sentiment damage from the Fed-driven sell-off.

Treasury yields eased across the curve: the 2-year note finished at 4.19% and the 10-year at 4.46%, down from the prior session's post-FOMC spike (when the 2-year had jumped to 4.21%), as the geopolitical risk premium embedded in oil prices unwound, pulling real yields lower and supporting the risk-on tone in equities.

Positioning confirmed the Fed's hawkish signal remained priced even after the rebound: CME Group's FedWatch tool showed traders assigning roughly a 50% probability to a 25-basis-point rate hike by September and about a 20% probability to a 50-basis-point hike, holding firm from the prior session's post-FOMC reprice even as risk assets rallied.

subject.name: June 18 sentiment driver
subject.event: US-Iran peace deal, chip rally, and Fed hawkish repricing

text: The mood shift had three reinforcing engines: first, the formal signing of the U.S.-Iran peace agreement at the Palace of Versailles and the lifting of the U.S. naval blockade on the Strait of Hormuz, which sent oil prices to their lowest since early March and eased inflation fears; second, a chip-sector surge after Trump announced an Apple-Intel manufacturing partnership that drove the PHLX semiconductor index up 6.4%; and third, softer Treasury yields that supported small caps and rate-sensitive equities, with SignatureFD CIO Tony Welch noting the 'package of data is still supportive whether or not the Fed has become a little bit more hawkish,' even as Cantor's Eric Johnston concluded 'the Fed has more credibility around inflation.'
